title: Rio De Janeiro Boat Rental: A Budget Guide for 2026
html:
Exploring Rio De Janeiro by boat need not break the bank; from Marina da Glória you can rent a motorboat for around BRL 600 for a half-day. BoatHire24 offers a trusted marketplace for comparing options and securing your booking.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Rio De Janeiro?

A shared tour costs from BRL 200 per person, while a private motorboat half-day starts at BRL 600. Prices vary by boat size, season, and crew. A small speedboat for two hours can be found for around BRL 400, whereas a full-day catamaran for up to 12 guests starts at BRL 2,500. For a crewed yacht, expect from BRL 4,000 for eight hours. One option worth a look is the Limpopo charter page.

Where to set off in Rio De Janeiro

Most boat trips depart from Marina da Glória, a central and well-served marina. Other options include the Yacht Club of Rio de Janeiro (ICRJ) in Botafogo, and the marina at Niterói across Guanabara Bay. From there you can head to Sugarloaf Mountain, Copacabana Beach, or the islands of Paquetá and Ilha Fiscal. Many skippered tours also visit the calm waters of Prainha and Grumari for snorkelling.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboat, ideal for small groups of 4-6 guests, half-day from BRL 600. Check the Limpopo charter page for a popular 28ft Axopar model.
  2. Sailboat, perfect for a relaxed 8-hour cruise with up to 8 guests, from BRL 1,200.
  3. Catamaran, spacious for parties of 10-12, full-day from BRL 2,500.
  4. Luxury motor yacht, such as a Maiora or Azimut, for up to 12 guests, full-day from BRL 5,000.
  5. RIB (rigid inflatable boat), fast and fun for exploring Gávea and Lagoa, half-day from BRL 450.

Cost breakdown and booking tips

Prices in Rio De Janeiro vary significantly. A shared tour on a schooner costs around BRL 200 per person for two hours. A small motorboat for half-day (4 hours) starts at BRL 600. A catamaran full-day (8 hours) for up to 12 guests is typically BRL 2,500. A crewed yacht with skipper and fuel can cost BRL 4,000 for a full day.

To find the best deals, consider booking through BoatHire24, where you can compare verified listings. Many operators include snorkelling gear and refreshments. Always confirm what is included, such as fuel, lunch, and port fees.

If you are considering a guided experience, browse skippered charters in Rio De Janeiro for options with local captains who know the best anchorages.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Rio De Janeiro?

For most small motorboats and sailboats under 20hp, no licence is required if a skipper is included. If you wish to self-drive, you may need a valid International Certificate of Competence (ICC). The operator will confirm exact requirements based on the boat size and power. For bareboat charters, a cruising licence is typically mandatory.

A useful tip from a local skipper: carry cash for tips and small purchases on the islands, many floating bars near Paquetá accept only cash.

Season and weather

The high season runs from December to March, with summer temperatures averaging 30°C. This period also brings stronger winds, particularly the south-westerly “ventania”. The best months for calm seas are April to October, though it is cooler. Book well in advance for December and January.

At a glance

  • Price band: BRL 200, 5,000+ per trip
  • Peak months: December to March
  • Trip length: 2 to 8 hours
  • Guest capacity: 4, 12 people
  • Currency: BRL (Brazilian real)

Questions about boat rental in Rio De Janeiro

Can I rent a boat without a skipper?

Yes, some operators offer bareboat charters for experienced skippers holding an ICC, but most rentals include a skipper.

What is the best time of year for a boat trip?

April to October provides milder weather and fewer crowds, while December to March offers hot sun and higher demand.

Are there boats for large groups?

Yes, catamarans and yachts can accommodate up to 12 guests; some operators allow larger groups on request.

What deposit is required?

Deposits vary but are typically 20-30% of the total, refundable if cancelled within the operator’s policy.

Whether you are planning a day on Guanabara Bay or an island-hopping adventure, BoatHire24 makes it simple to book and compare options. If you own a boat in Rio De Janeiro, you can also become a host and earn income from your vessel. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Rio De Janeiro or list your own.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Rio De Janeiro and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
